description: Writing a field of a binding in a NON-terminal substate does not fire E-STATE-TERMINAL-MUTATION (§54.6.4). `d` is typed `Draft`, which declares the transition `validate()` (non-terminal), so `d.body = "x"` is not a terminal-state mutation and the check stays silent. The clean twin of e-state-terminal-mutation-pos (E-TYPE-UNKNOWN-NAME + W-WHITESPACE-001 co-fire incidentally from the canonical substate syntax and are left out of notCodes).
rationale: §54.6.4 terminality is defined by having zero outgoing transitions; a substate with at least one declared transition (`Draft` → `validate()`) is non-terminal, so a field write on it does not fire E-STATE-TERMINAL-MUTATION.
